title: Ultrafiltration Methods for Concentrating and Detecting Salmonella Typhi and Salmonella Paratyphi A in Wastewater Samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5cai2","text":"Tangential flow ultrafiltration is used to concentrate large volumes of environmental water samples (e.g., wastewater, wastewater-impacted surface waters, etc.) for detection of S. Typhi and other microorganisms, using molecular methods. This method is applicable for sites with high flow rates, as a large volume is required. Results are quantifiable when enrichment is not used. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":101,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":178,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a2d2u","text":"This method was developed at Emory University, and it has been previously described in the following articles (Liu P et al, J Microbiol Methods 2012; Liu P et al, J Appl Micobiology 2013; Amin N et al, Intern J Hygene Environ Health 2020).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":124,"length":19},{"style":"italic","offset":163,"length":18},{"style":"italic","offset":200,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54mr9","text":"A 40-L grab sample is collected from the source water using a bucket. The sample is brought to the lab and concentrated through a tangential flow ultrafilter. S. Typhi and S. Paratyphi A are captured on the filter by size exclusion, and eluted in ~500 ml volume of eluant buffer. The eluate is further concentrated by polyethylene (PEG) precipitation. The final pellet is extracted directly for molecular testing. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":159,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Pengbo Liu
